[[Category:Cases]]
The '''Instrumental case''' in Aelaki marks the instrument used to perform an action. It also functions as a '''comitative''' (companion, "with") and '''locative''' (location, "at/in") case.

== Formation ==

The Instrumental case is formed by adding a ''-ko'' or ''-kto'' type suffix to the noun, with the exact form varying by person. The inanimate form uses the ''-kïf'' suffix with vowel harmony.

== Declension ==

Demonstrated with the feminine singular noun '''basol''' ("tree"):

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Form
|-
| 1st person || Basolothko
|-
| 2nd person || Basolozhgo
|-
| 3rd person || Basoloshko
|-
| 4th person || Basolokto
|-
| Inanimate || Basïlkïf
|}

== Multiple Functions ==

The Instrumental case serves three semantic roles:

* '''Instrumental''' - the tool or means by which an action is performed
* '''Comitative''' - the companion or entity accompanying the subject ("with")
* '''Locative''' - the location where an action takes place ("at", "in")

Context and the verb determine which reading applies in a given sentence.

== Case Hierarchy ==

The Instrumental case ranks below [[Patient]] in the case hierarchy for verb agreement:

[[Agent]] > [[Dative]] > [[Patient]] > '''Instrumental''' > [[Possessive]] > [[Speaker]]

== See Also ==

* [[Agent]] - marks the subject of transitive/active verbs
* [[Patient]] - marks the object of transitive verbs
* [[Dative]] - marks the recipient or beneficiary
* [[Speaker]] - marks the source of reported information
* [[Possessive]] - marks genitive constructions
